description: In C++, use the package-local commands (CMake targets (cmake --build) with the configured generator and CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE) rather than a root or generic command.
body: In C++, the real contract lives in the owning package's config; a root or generic command can use the wrong the compiler, standard library (libstdc++/libc++), and CMake version or skip checks. Use when building, testing, or analyzing a specific C++ package, especially in a monorepo. Do not assume a top-level command covers the package, and avoid running a generic command that ignores its config. Actions: find the package's own targets, run CMake targets (cmake --build) with the configured generator and CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE and the ctest suite plus a sanitized build of the touched target scoped to it, and compare results against the package's configured gate. Validation signals include the package-scoped command log, its exit code, and a clang-tidy, include-what-you-use, and -Wpedantic diagnostics pass using the package config. Risk boundary: escalate if no package-local command exists or if it depends on environment you cannot safely provision.
